questions.question: What is the smallest odd number in Whole Numbers?
questions.explanation: Whole Numbers include all positive integers, starting from 0. The smallest odd number in this set is indeed 1.

questions.question: What property does the additive identity possess?
questions.explanation: Additive identity is a concept where adding zero to any number leaves that number unchanged.

questions.question: What is the main component of an air conditioner?
questions.explanation: The refrigerant absorbs heat from indoor air and releases it outside, making spaces more comfortable.

questions.question: What does Pascal's Triangle represent?
questions.explanation: Each entry in the triangle is the sum of the two numbers directly above it, representing binomial coefficients.

questions.question: How many sides does a regular polygon have?
questions.explanation: A regular hexagon has six equal sides and angles.

questions.question: What is the smallest even number in Whole Numbers?
questions.explanation: Whole Numbers include all positive integers, starting from 0. The smallest even number in this set is indeed 0.

questions.question: What symbol represents infinity?
questions.explanation: The lemniscate symbol was introduced to represent a quantity that continues forever.

questions.question: How many rows are in Pascal's Triangle?
questions.explanation: Pascal's Triangle has an infinite number of rows, each representing binomial coefficients.

questions.question: What is the main purpose of a metal detector?
questions.explanation: Metal detectors generate an electromagnetic field to find metal objects underground or inside other materials.

questions.question: Who invented the first modern air conditioner?
questions.explanation: Carrier introduced the concept of cooling and dehumidifying indoor air with his invention in 1902.

notes.title: Additive Identity Property
notes.content: The additive identity is a number that, when added to any other number, leaves the second number unchanged. In Whole Numbers, this property is represented as a + 0 = a.

notes.title: Properties of Infinity
notes.content: Infinity represents values that grow beyond any fixed number and signifies eternity, balance, and the interconnection of all things.

notes.title: Pascal's Triangle Structure
notes.content: Each entry in Pascal's Triangle is the sum of the two numbers directly above it, representing binomial coefficients.

notes.title: Whole Numbers Definition
notes.content: Whole Numbers include all positive integers, starting from 0.

notes.title: Air Conditioner Functioning
notes.content: An air conditioner works by using a refrigeration cycle to absorb heat from indoor air and release it outside.

facts.fact: The first metal detector was invented to find a bullet in President Garfield!
Pascal's Triangle has many fascinating properties, including representing binomial coefficients and Fibonacci numbers.
Infinity is often used in jewelry to represent everlasting love!
The first modern air conditioner was invented by Willis Carrier in 1902.
Pascal's Triangle is used in algebra, probability, and combinatorics to find binomial coefficients.
